data.body:
PASS 1 review (criterion T1 — Prior-art / novel-architecture justification): no findings.

T1 is an overlay that applies only when a bright-line fires (external integration, unfamiliar dependency, security/auth mechanism, novel architecture, perf/scalability target, or migration). This ticket is documentation-only ("This is documentation only — no behavior changes") describing the reuse surface of EXISTING, well-trodden subsystems (rebar.signing, rebar.llm.runner/workflow, rebar.llm.prompts, rebar.llm.findings). No new pattern, dependency, or mechanism is introduced, so no bright-line fires — T1 is not-applicable here.

Affirmations against T1's checklist:
- Prior art is explicitly considered, not reinvented: the plan grounds itself in the existing framework/usage/event docs (docs/llm-framework.md, docs/event-schema.md, docs/workflow-authoring-v2.md) and frames the new reference as filling a documented gap rather than repackaging them.
- No novel pattern is proposed — it documents established machinery — so the Rule-of-Three / anti-repackaging concern does not apply.
- No unverified capability assertions: AC ("Each documented signature/return shape is verified against the current source — no drift") commits to grounding every claim in real source rather than asserting unverified capabilities.
